name: Gamaredon 2024 evolved-toolset spearphishing campaigns against Ukrainian government
actor: NEW: Gamaredon / Shuckworm / Aqua Blizzard (FSB 18th Center)
summary: ESET's July 2025 'Gamaredon in 2024' report documents the FSB-linked Gamaredon group intensifying spearphishing against Ukrainian governmental institutions through 2024, deploying six new PowerShell/VBScript tools (PteroDespair, PteroTickle, PteroGraphin, PteroStew, PteroQuark, PteroBox), hiding nearly all C2 behind Cloudflare tunnels, and using malicious LNK/XHTML HTML-smuggling delivery. Gamaredon is one of the most operationally active FSB cyber actors against Ukraine yet has no events in the current corpus, making this a high-leverage backfill. Attributed by SSU to the 18th Center of Information Security of Russia's FSB. Maps to ru/gerasimov-framework (whole-of-war information confrontation) or ru/russkiy-mir.
supporting_quote: Gamaredon, attributed by the Security Service of Ukraine (SSU) to the 18th Center of Information Security of Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB)
notes: ESET page loads and confirms all specifics: SSU-to-FSB-18th-Center attribution, exclusive 2024 Ukrainian-gov targeting, all six named Ptero tools, and Cloudflare-tunnel C2. No Gamaredon/Shuckworm/Aqua Blizzard entry exists in RU corpus — not a duplicate.

name: APT28 GooseEgg post-compromise tool exploiting CVE-2022-38028 (Print Spooler)
summary: Microsoft Threat Intelligence (22 Apr 2024) disclosed GooseEgg, a custom Forest Blizzard / APT28 post-compromise launcher used since at least June 2020 to exploit Windows Print Spooler CVE-2022-38028, escalating to SYSTEM to steal credentials and enable lateral movement and backdoor installation. US/UK governments link Forest Blizzard to GRU Unit 26165. Targets span Ukrainian, Western European, and North American government, NGO, education, and transportation organizations. Distinct from the APT28 router/DNS and CVE-2023-23397 events already in the corpus. Maps to ru/military-doctrine-2014 / ru/gerasimov-framework.
supporting_quote: The United States and United Kingdom governments have linked Forest Blizzard to Unit 26165 of the Russian Federation's military intelligence agency, the Main Intelligence Directorate of the General Staff of the Armed Forces of the Russian Federation (GRU).
notes: Microsoft page loads and confirms the GooseEgg tool, CVE-2022-38028 Print Spooler exploitation, GRU Unit 26165 attribution, and Ukraine/W.Europe/N.America targeting. Distinct from the existing 2024 Moobot-router and DNS-hijacking and 2022 CVE-2023-23397 APT28 corpus entries — not a duplicate.

name: Turla TinyTurla-NG backdoor espionage against Polish NGO supporting Ukraine
summary: Cisco Talos (Feb 2024) attributed a new 'last-chance' backdoor TinyTurla-NG and the TurlaPower-NG PowerShell exfiltrators to the Russian cyber-espionage group Turla, used against a Polish NGO working to improve Polish democracy and support Ukraine during the Russian invasion. Earliest compromise 18 Dec 2023, active through 27 Jan 2024; C2 via compromised WordPress sites. Turla is currently a singleton in the corpus (only Moonlight Maze); a second well-sourced event makes it LOO-rankable. Maps to ru/gerasimov-framework / ru/sanctions-response-2022 (monitoring Western aid to Ukraine).
supporting_quote: TinyTurla-NG was seen as early as December 2023 targeting a Polish non-governmental organization (NGO) working on improving Polish democracy and supporting Ukraine during the Russian invasion.
notes: Talos page loads and confirms TinyTurla-NG + TurlaPower-NG attributed to Turla, Polish-NGO/Ukraine target, Dec 18 2023 earliest compromise active to Jan 27 2024, and compromised-WordPress C2. RU corpus has only the 1996 Moonlight Maze Turla entry — not a duplicate.

name: Secret Blizzard (Turla) ApolloShadow ISP-level AitM surveillance of Moscow embassies
summary: Microsoft Threat Intelligence (disclosed late July 2025) reported that Secret Blizzard (Turla), affiliated with FSB Center 16, has since at least 2024 maintained adversary-in-the-middle positions at the Russian ISP/telecom level to surveil foreign diplomats in Moscow, deploying custom ApolloShadow malware via a fake Kaspersky-branded certificate-validation lure that installs rogue root certificates, disables TLS encryption, and grants persistent access to diplomatic devices. A novel domestic ISP-position diplomatic-surveillance operation distinct from anything in the corpus. Maps to ru/info-security-2016 / ru/gerasimov-framework.
supporting_quote: A Russian nation-state threat group has been spying on foreign diplomats, managing continuous access to their communications and data in Moscow since at least 2024, according to Microsoft Threat Intelligence.
notes: CyberScoop page loads and confirms Secret Blizzard/Turla / FSB Center 16, ISP/telecom adversary-in-the-middle on Russian networks since at least 2024, ApolloShadow malware via fake-Kaspersky root-cert lure, Moscow diplomats. Distinct from the TinyTurla-NG candidate and from the BY MoustachedBouncer (2014) entry — not a duplicate.

name: COLDRIVER / Star Blizzard LOSTKEYS document-theft malware campaign
summary: Google Threat Intelligence Group (May 2025) disclosed LOSTKEYS, new malware attributed to the Russian government-backed group COLDRIVER (Star Blizzard / Callisto / UNC4057), observed January, March, and April 2025. Delivered via a ClickFix-style fake-CAPTCHA lure, LOSTKEYS steals files from hard-coded extensions/directories and exfiltrates system info and running processes; deployed only in highly selective cases. Recent targets: current/former advisors to Western governments and militaries, journalists, think tanks, NGOs, and individuals connected to Ukraine. The existing Star Blizzard corpus entry is a meta attribution event; this is a distinct malware-and-targeting operation. Maps to ru/gerasimov-framework / ru/fpc-2023.
supporting_quote: Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has identified a new piece of malware called LOSTKEYS, attributed to the Russian government-backed threat group COLDRIVER (also known as UNC4057, Star Blizzard, and Callisto).
notes: Google Cloud GTIG page loads and confirms LOSTKEYS attributed to COLDRIVER/Star Blizzard/Callisto/UNC4057, Jan/Mar/Apr 2025 observation, ClickFix fake-CAPTCHA delivery, file-theft + system recon, and Western-advisor/journalist/think-tank/NGO/Ukraine targeting. Distinct from the 2015 Star Blizzard meta-attribution and 2022 COLDRIVER phishing-wave corpus entries — not a duplicate.

name: Ghostwriter / UAC-0057 PicassoLoader campaign against Belarusian opposition and Ukraine (Nov 2024-)
summary: SentinelLABS (25 Feb 2025) documented a Ghostwriter (UNC1151 / UAC-0057) campaign — in preparation since July-August 2024, active phase November-December 2024 and continuing into early 2025 — deploying a simplified PicassoDownloader / PicassoLoader variant via malicious Excel macros against Belarusian opposition activists and Ukrainian military and government organizations, using lures on 'political prisoners,' an 'Anti-Corruption Initiative,' and 'Supplies for Ukraine Armed Forces.' SentinelLABS assesses Ghostwriter is closely tied to Belarusian government interests. Distinct from the 2023-07 Ghostwriter PicassoLoader corpus entry. Maps to by/concept-info-security-2019 / by/union-state-integration.
supporting_quote: The actor behind Ghostwriter campaigns is closely linked with Belarusian government espionage efforts, while most commonly reported under the APT names UNC1151 (Mandiant) or UAC-0057 (CERT-UA).
notes: SentinelLABS page loads and confirms the July-Aug 2024 prep / Nov-Dec 2024 active phase, PicassoDownloader/PicassoLoader via weaponized Excel VBA macros, Belarusian-opposition + Ukrainian-military/gov targeting, named lures, and Belarusian-government linkage. Distinct from the existing 2023-07 Ghostwriter PicassoLoader BY entry — not a duplicate.

name: Belarusian Cyber Partisans breach and prolonged disruption of Belarus KGB network
summary: On 27 Apr 2024 the anti-Lukashenko hacktivist group Belarusian Cyber Partisans announced they had penetrated the Belarus KGB network in fall 2023, exfiltrating personnel files for more than 8,600 KGB employees and suppressing/taking the KGB's official website offline for over two months. They published administrator lists, the underlying database, and server logs as proof. This is an anti-regime operation against the Belarusian state (the actor opposes, and is not sponsored by, the state). Distinct from the corpus's 2021 Lukashenko-database leak and 2022 BZhD railway events. Maps to by/concept-info-security-2019 as a counter-doctrine challenge to regime information control.
supporting_quote: In April 2024, Cyber Partisans announced they had penetrated the Belarus KGB computer network in fall 2023, accessing files of 8,600 Belarus KGB employees, and beginning in February 2024, they suppressed the Belarus KGB computer network for at least two months.
notes: SecurityAffairs page loads and confirms the Cyber Partisans' fall-2023 KGB-network breach, 8,600-employee personnel files, published admin-list/database/server-logs, and 2+ month KGB-website outage announced April 2024. Distinct from the 2022 BZhD railway BCP entry in the BY corpus — not a duplicate.

name: Aeroflot destructive cyberattack — Silent Crow + Belarusian Cyber Partisans wipe ~7,000 servers
summary: On 28 July 2025 pro-Ukrainian group Silent Crow and the Belarusian Cyber Partisans claimed a destructive intrusion of Russian flag carrier Aeroflot, stating they had disabled around 7,000 internal servers and stolen up to 12-22 TB of data (incl. passenger records from Aeroflot, Pobeda, Rossiya) after roughly a year of access gained via the director's unchanged 2022 password and legacy Windows XP/2003 systems. The attack forced cancellation of dozens-to-100+ flights including domestic and international routes to Belarus, Armenia and Uzbekistan. A major anti-regime destructive operation by Belarus-linked hacktivists against a Russian state-owned target (actor opposes, and is not sponsored by, the state); co-claimant Silent Crow is a possible NEW actor. Maps to by/concept-info-security-2019 / cross-references ru/sanctions-response-2022 victimology.
supporting_quote: Silent Crow and the Belarusian Cyber Partisans – have claimed responsibility for the attack, according to reports.
notes: Bitdefender page loads and confirms Silent Crow + Belarusian Cyber Partisans claim, ~7,000 servers disabled, up to 12TB stolen (Aeroflot/Pobeda/Rossiya passenger records), ~1 year of access, and dozens-to-100+ flight cancellations. Source does not name the Belarus/Armenia/Uzbekistan routes (peripheral detail), but the core event is fully supported. Not present in RU or BY corpus — not a duplicate.

name: DOJ indicts 12 Chinese contract hackers incl. i-Soon (Anxun), MPS officers, and APT27 operators
summary: On 5 March 2025 the US DOJ unsealed charges against 12 Chinese nationals — eight employees of the ostensibly private PRC firm Anxun Information Technology (i-Soon), two officers of the Ministry of Public Security (MPS), and two members of APT27 (Silk Typhoon; Yin Kecheng and Zhou Shuai). The indictments describe i-Soon as a key node in the PRC hacker-for-hire ecosystem that conducted intrusions at the direction of the MSS/MPS and also sold stolen data to dozens of provincial MSS/MPS bureaus. This is the criminal-indictment counterpart to the February 2024 i-Soon document leak already in the corpus, formally tying the contractor model to PRC intelligence tasking and transnational repression.
supporting_quote: The DOJ said i-Soon generated millions of dollars as key cogs in China's hacker-for-hire ecosystem — often taking direction from China's MPS or Ministry of State Security (MSS).
notes: Source confirms 5 March 2025 DOJ charges against 12 Chinese nationals — 8 i-Soon employees (incl. CEO Wu Haibo, COO Chen Cheng), 2 MPS officers, and APT27/Silk Typhoon operators Yin Kecheng and Zhou Shuai; distinct from the 2024-02-16 i-Soon leak and the 2025-01-17 OFAC action already in corpus.

name: APT41 TOUGHPROGRESS malware using Google Calendar for C2
summary: Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) disclosed on 28 May 2025 that in late October 2024 it found a compromised government website delivering a novel malware payload, TOUGHPROGRESS, that abused Google Calendar events as its command-and-control channel. GTIG attributed the campaign with high confidence to the PRC actor APT41 (HOODOO), which targeted government entities plus the global shipping/logistics, media/entertainment, technology, and automotive sectors via spearphishing. GTIG disrupted the operation by fingerprinting and removing the attacker-controlled Calendars and Workspace projects. Distinct from the APT41 'DUST' campaign already in the corpus.
supporting_quote: We assess with high confidence that this malware is being used by the PRC based actor APT41 (also tracked as HOODOO).
notes: GTIG blog (28 May 2025) confirms TOUGHPROGRESS malware using Google Calendar for C2, found on a compromised govt site in late Oct 2024, attributed with high confidence to APT41/HOODOO; sectors and disruption match. Distinct from 2023 APT41 DUST entry.

name: Salt Typhoon compromise of a US state's Army National Guard network
summary: A DHS memo (dated 11 June 2025) summarizing Pentagon findings, reported 16 July 2025, found that Salt Typhoon extensively compromised a US state's Army National Guard network between March and December 2024, exfiltrating administrator credentials, network configuration files, network-traffic diagrams with counterpart units in every other US state and at least four territories, geographic location maps, and service members' PII. Officials assessed the access could enable follow-on intrusions and undermine state-level critical-infrastructure defense (Guard units integrated with fusion centers). Distinct from the tier-1 telecom Salt Typhoon entries and the AA25-239A advisory already in the corpus.
supporting_quote: Salt Typhoon used its access to a US state's Army National Guard network to exfiltrate administrator credentials, network traffic diagrams, a map of geographic locations throughout the state and [personally identifiable information] of its service members.
notes: Nextgov (16 July 2025) confirms DHS I&A memo (dated 11 June 2025) finding Salt Typhoon compromised a US state's Army National Guard network March–December 2024, exfiltrating admin creds, traffic diagrams (with every other state + 4 territories), geo maps, and service members' PII. Distinct target from telecom Salt Typhoon entries and AA25-239a.

name: China-aligned phishing campaigns against Taiwan's semiconductor industry (UNK_FistBump / UNK_DropPitch / UNK_SparkyCarp)
actor: NEW: China-aligned Taiwan semiconductor espionage cluster (UNK_FistBump / UNK_DropPitch / UNK_SparkyCarp)
summary: Proofpoint Threat Research (published 16 July 2025) reported that between March and June 2025 three distinct China-aligned/state-sponsored threat actors — tracked as UNK_FistBump, UNK_DropPitch, and UNK_SparkyCarp — ran targeted phishing campaigns against Taiwan's semiconductor industry: manufacturing, design and testing firms, the wider equipment/services supply chain, and financial analysts covering the sector. UNK_FistBump used employment-themed lures to deliver Cobalt Strike or the custom Voldemort backdoor (Google Sheets C2 via CiscoCollabHost DLL sideloading). Assessed motive is espionage aligned with PRC semiconductor self-sufficiency goals under export controls — directly on the Taiwan / IP-theft focus and not in the corpus.
supporting_quote: Between March and June 2025, Proofpoint Threat Research observed three Chinese state-sponsored threat actors conduct targeted phishing campaigns against the Taiwanese semiconductor industry.
notes: Proofpoint blog (16 July 2025) confirms three Chinese state-sponsored actors UNK_FistBump/UNK_DropPitch/UNK_SparkyCarp phishing Taiwan's semiconductor industry March–June 2025, with Cobalt Strike and custom Voldemort backdoor. New actor cluster, not in corpus.

name: TA415 (Brass Typhoon / APT41-linked) US-China economic-relations espionage via VS Code Remote Tunnels
summary: Proofpoint (published 16 September 2025) detailed that throughout July-August 2025 TA415 — a China-aligned actor Proofpoint links publicly to APT41 / Brass Typhoon — ran spearphishing campaigns against US government, think-tank, and academic organizations using US-China economic-relations lures (impersonating US officials and trade bodies). The actor adopted VS Code Remote Tunnels as a primary persistence/remote-access mechanism, enabling hands-on-keyboard access without traditional malware, to collect intelligence on US trade policy and economic negotiations. Maps to the existing APT41 actor but is a distinct 2025 economic-collection campaign not represented in the corpus.
supporting_quote: Throughout July and August 2025, TA415 conducted spearphishing campaigns targeting United States government, think tank, and academic organizations utilizing U.S.-China economic-themed lures.
notes: Proofpoint blog (16 Sept 2025) confirms TA415 (overlaps APT41/Brass Typhoon/Wicked Panda, US-indicted 2020) spearphishing US govt/think-tank/academic orgs in July–Aug 2025 with US-China economic lures (impersonating Select Committee chair and US-China Business Council) and establishing VS Code Remote Tunnels for malware-free persistence. Distinct 2025 campaign.

name: LapDogs — China-nexus Operational Relay Box (ORB) network of compromised SOHO/edge devices
summary: SecurityScorecard's STRIKE team (June 2025 report) disclosed LapDogs, a suspected China-Nexus Operational Relay Box (ORB) network that has compromised over 1,000 Linux-based Small Office/Home Office (SOHO) and edge devices since September 2023, concentrated in the US and Southeast Asia (Japan, South Korea, Hong Kong, Taiwan). It uses a custom backdoor 'ShortLeash' that installs self-signed TLS certificates with spoofed metadata; Mandarin developer notes, TTPs, and victimology support China-nexus APT attribution. LapDogs provides covert relay infrastructure to obscure espionage operations and shares some traits with the PolarEdge ORB. A distinct edge-device botnet/ORB not in the corpus (separate from Raptor Train / Flax Typhoon).
supporting_quote: SecurityScorecard's STRIKE research team has identified a new suspected China-Nexus network of Operational Relay Boxes (ORB) called "LapDogs" targeting primarily Linux-based Small Office/Home Office (SOHO) devices around the globe.
notes: PDF verified via direct image read (WebFetch could not parse binary); Executive Summary + Key Takeaways confirm China-Nexus ORB 'LapDogs', 1,000+ Linux SOHO nodes since Sept 2023, ShortLeash backdoor with spoofed self-signed TLS certs, Mandarin developer notes, US/SE-Asia (Japan, S.Korea, HK, Taiwan) targeting, and PolarEdge sister-ORB. Distinct from Raptor Train/Flax Typhoon.

name: Lazarus Operation DreamJob targets European UAV/drone defense firms (ScoringMathTea)
summary: ESET disclosed an Operation DreamJob wave in which North Korea-aligned Lazarus targeted at least three European defense-industry companies in Central and Southeastern Europe — a metal-engineering firm, an aircraft-components manufacturer, and a defense contractor — several heavily involved in the unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) sector, including a producer of UAV models currently deployed in Ukraine. Victims were lured with fake job offers carrying a trojanized PDF reader that deployed the ScoringMathTea RAT (with BinMergeLoader/MISTPEN-style and QuanPinLoader components). ESET assesses the primary goal was theft of proprietary information and UAV manufacturing know-how, aligning with DPRK efforts to scale domestic drone capability. Distinct from the 2022 Spain-aerospace LightlessCan event already in the corpus.
supporting_quote: Lazarus targeted several companies active in the defense industry in Central and Southeastern Europe. Some of these are heavily involved in the unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV / drones) sector.
notes: ESET page confirms Lazarus DreamJob targeting Central/SE European defense firms in UAV sector from late March 2025; names ScoringMathTea, BinMergeLoader, QuanPinLoader; goal = stealing UAV proprietary info/know-how. Matches actor, date, mechanism. Not a dup of 2022 Spain LightlessCan.

name: UNC1069 (North Korea) compromises axios npm package in supply-chain attack (WAVESHAPER.V2)
actor: NEW: UNC1069 (BlueNoroff / Sapphire Sleet / Stardust Chollima overlap)
summary: Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) attributed a supply-chain compromise of the widely used axios npm package (100M+ weekly downloads) to UNC1069, a financially motivated North Korea-nexus actor. On 31 March 2026 the attacker pushed malicious axios releases (1.14.1 and 0.30.4) containing a malicious dependency 'plain-crypto-js' (SILKBELL dropper) that deployed OS-specific WAVESHAPER.V2 backdoors for Windows, macOS, and Linux, supporting reconnaissance, command execution, and PE injection. The maintainer account was taken over via tailored social engineering. UNC1069 overlaps with BlueNoroff/Sapphire Sleet/Stardust Chollima but is tracked distinctly by Google; not currently in the corpus.
supporting_quote: GTIG attributes this activity to UNC1069, a financially motivated North Korea-nexus threat actor active since at least 2018, based on the use of WAVESHAPER.V2, an updated version of WAVESHAPER previously used by this threat actor.
notes: Google Cloud GTIG page confirms UNC1069 (NK-nexus) compromise of axios npm (1.14.1 / 0.30.4) on 31 Mar 2026; names plain-crypto-js, SILKBELL dropper, WAVESHAPER.V2 across Win/macOS/Linux; maintainer account takeover (email changed to attacker-controlled). New actor not in corpus; distinct from 2024 Contagious Interview npm campaign.

name: Moonstone Sleet deploys custom FakePenny ransomware ($6.6M BTC demand)
summary: Microsoft Threat Intelligence disclosed that the North Korean actor Moonstone Sleet (formerly Storm-1789) delivered a new custom ransomware variant, FakePenny, in April 2024 against a company it had previously compromised in February, demanding a $6.6M USD ransom in BTC — the group's first observed ransomware deployment. Moonstone Sleet also ran fake companies (StarGlow Ventures, C.C. Waterfall), a trojanized tank game (DeTankWar), and IT-worker schemes, blending espionage with revenue generation. This is a distinct operational event from the August 2023 MSTIC outing of Moonstone Sleet already in the corpus (which is the only existing Moonstone Sleet entry).
supporting_quote: In April 2024, Microsoft observed Moonstone Sleet delivering a new custom ransomware variant we have named FakePenny against a company it previously compromised in February... The ransom demand was $6.6M USD in BTC.
notes: Microsoft blog confirms verbatim: Moonstone Sleet (ex-Storm-1789) deployed FakePenny ransomware April 2024, $6.6M BTC demand, first ransomware op; also confirms StarGlow Ventures, C.C. Waterfall, DeTankWar, IT-worker scheme. Distinct operational event from 2023-08 MSTIC outing.

name: Kelp DAO rsETH LayerZero bridge exploit — ~$292M DPRK theft
summary: TRM Labs attributed (via on-chain analysis of pre-funding and laundering) a ~$292M theft from Kelp DAO, a restaking protocol, to North Korea on 18 April 2026. Attackers compromised two internal RPC nodes and DDoSed legitimate nodes, forcing Kelp DAO's single-verifier rsETH LayerZero bridge to confirm a fraudulent cross-chain message. With the Drift Protocol theft (April 1, already in the corpus), DPRK accounted for ~76% of all 2026 crypto-hack value. crypto.news ties both attacks to Lazarus/TraderTraitor; this Kelp DAO event is distinct from Drift and not yet in the corpus.
supporting_quote: TRM Labs' has attributed this exploit to North Korea based on on-chain analysis of both the pre-funding for the hack and the subsequent laundering.
notes: TRM Labs page confirms ~$292M KelpDAO (rsETH) theft on 18 Apr 2026 attributed to NK via on-chain pre-funding/laundering (funding traced to Wu Huihui, indicted Lazarus launderer); confirms compromised RPC nodes + DDoS on single-verifier LayerZero bridge, and ~76%-of-2026 figure alongside Drift. Distinct from Drift event in corpus.

name: DOJ indicts DPRK nationals Jin Sung-Il and Pak Jin-Song in IT-worker fraud scheme
summary: On 23 January 2025 DOJ unsealed an indictment charging two North Korean nationals — Jin Sung-Il and Pak Jin-Song — plus three facilitators (Mexican national Pedro Ernesto Alonso De Los Reyes and U.S. citizens Erick Ntekereze Prince and Emanuel Ashtor) for a multi-year (April 2018–August 2024) fraudulent remote-IT-worker scheme. Using forged/stolen identities, laptop farms, and remote-access software, they obtained work from at least 64 U.S. companies; ten generated at least $866,255 laundered through a Chinese bank account. Charges include CFAA, wire/mail-fraud, money-laundering, false-ID, and (for Jin/Pak) IEEPA sanctions violations. This is the first indictment to charge actual DPRK nationals in the IT-worker scheme — distinct from the facilitator-only entries already in the corpus. (Govt primary justice.gov returned 403; verified via The Hacker News reporting on the DOJ release.)
supporting_quote: Payments from ten of those companies generated at least $866,255 in revenue, most of which the defendants then laundered through a Chinese bank account.
notes: The Hacker News page confirms 23 Jan 2025 DOJ indictment of DPRK nationals Jin Sung-Il & Pak Jin-Song plus three named facilitators; Apr 2018–Aug 2024, 64 US companies, $866,255 laundered via Chinese bank, CFAA/wire-fraud/IEEPA charges. Distinct from 2017 14-national indictment and facilitator-only corpus entries.

name: BlueNoroff 'Hidden Risk' macOS campaign against crypto/DeFi firms (novel Zshenv persistence)
summary: SentinelOne (SentinelLABS) disclosed the 'Hidden Risk' campaign — attributed with high confidence to BlueNoroff (suspected DPRK-affiliated, Lazarus subcluster) — targeting cryptocurrency and DeFi businesses on macOS from around July 2024. Email/PDF lures with fake crypto-news headlines delivered a Swift dropper displaying a decoy PDF and a C++ backdoor ('growth'), using a first-in-the-wild persistence technique that abuses the Zshenv configuration file. Aligns with an FBI warning that the DPRK runs tailored social-engineering against DeFi/crypto employees to steal company cryptocurrency. Distinct from the 2022 BlueNoroff VC-impersonation MoTW event already in the corpus (BlueNoroff is currently a singleton there).
supporting_quote: We assess with high confidence that the same actor is responsible for earlier attacks attributed to BlueNoroff.
notes: SentinelLABS page confirms BlueNoroff 'Hidden Risk' macOS campaign from ~July 2024 vs crypto/DeFi; fake crypto-news PDF lure, Swift dropper, C++ 'growth' backdoor, and first-in-the-wild Zshenv persistence abuse. Distinct from 2022 VC-impersonation MoTW entry.

name: Kimsuky (APT43) GitHub-C2 XenoRAT campaign against embassies in Seoul
summary: Trellix documented a spear-phishing campaign (March–July 2025) against foreign embassies in Seoul — generally European facilities — using multilingual lures (Korean, English, Persian, Arabic, French, Russian) with fake meeting invites and official letters. Password-protected ZIPs hid LNK files that deployed a XenoRAT variant, with GitHub abused as a covert C2 channel and Dropbox/Daum for delivery, enabling keylogging, screenshots, webcam/mic access, and remote shell. ATTRIBUTION CAVEAT: Trellix attributes to Kimsuky/APT43 only at medium confidence and hypothesizes possible Chinese sponsorship/involvement (attacker working hours and holiday pauses align with a China-based actor) — hence 'plausible' rather than attested. Not currently in the corpus. (Trellix primary returned 403; verified via Bleeping Computer reporting on the Trellix research.)
supporting_quote: Trellix concludes that the campaign is attributed to APT43 with medium confidence, hypothesizing some form of Chinese sponsorship or involvement.
notes: BleepingComputer page (reporting Trellix) confirms XenoRAT spear-phishing of foreign embassies in Seoul Mar–Jul 2025, multilingual lures, password-protected ZIP/LNK, GitHub C2 + Dropbox/Daum delivery, APT43/Kimsuky at medium confidence with Chinese-involvement hypothesis. Attribution caveat preserved as 'plausible'. Not in corpus.

name: Scarred Manticore LIONTAIL espionage campaign across Middle East and Israel
supporting_quote: Scarred Manticore, an actor affiliated with the Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS)
summary: Check Point Research (with Sygnia) disclosed Scarred Manticore, an Iranian MOIS-affiliated actor (overlapping OilRig/APT34 and the DEV-0861 cluster behind the 2021 Albania attack), running a stealthy long-dwell espionage campaign against government, military, telecommunications, financial and IT-services targets across Saudi Arabia, UAE, Jordan, Kuwait, Oman, Iraq and Israel. The campaign used the custom LIONTAIL passive-implant framework, which abuses undocumented HTTP.sys driver functionality to extract memory-resident payloads from inbound HTTP traffic. NOT currently in the inventory; this is the upstream espionage half of the Scarred Manticore/Void Manticore handoff pair.
notes: CP page (pub 2023-10-31) confirms Scarred Manticore as MOIS-affiliated, LIONTAIL passive framework abusing HTTP.sys, campaign peaked mid-2023 vs gov/military/telecom/financial/NGO across Saudi/UAE/Jordan/Kuwait/Oman/Iraq/Israel; no existing IR entry for this actor.

name: Void Manticore BiBi-wiper destructive operations against Israel via Karma persona
supporting_quote: Void Manticore is an Iranian threat actor affiliated with the Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS).
summary: Check Point disclosed Void Manticore (aka Storm-842), an Iranian MOIS-affiliated destructive actor operating the 'Karma' hacktivist persona against Israel and 'Homeland Justice' against Albania. It deploys the custom BiBi wiper (named after PM Netanyahu) in Windows and Linux variants plus partition wipers (JustMBR, LowEraser, Pinky), claiming 40+ Israeli victims via data theft, wiping and leaks. Notably, Check Point documents a systematic target handoff: Scarred Manticore exfiltrates over long dwell, then transfers access to Void Manticore for the destructive phase. Distinct from the inventory's existing HomeLand Justice and Handala entries; ties to the Albania/HomeLand Justice lineage but is a separately-tracked MOIS destructive unit.
notes: CP page (pub 2024-05-20) confirms Void Manticore as MOIS-affiliated, BiBi wiper named after Netanyahu (Win/Linux), Karma persona for Israel + Homeland Justice for Albania, and explicit Scarred Manticore-to-Void Manticore access handoff; distinct from existing HomeLand Justice/Handala entries.

name: Agrius / Agonizing Serpens wiper attacks on Israeli education and tech sectors
supporting_quote: Agonizing Serpens (aka Agrius) is an Iranian-linked APT group that has been active since 2020.
summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 detailed destructive attacks (Jan-Oct 2023) by Agonizing Serpens (aka Agrius, Pink Sandstorm/AMERICIUM, BlackShadow, DEV-0022, SPECTRAL KITTEN) against Israeli higher-education and technology organizations. The group stole PII and IP, then deployed novel wipers MultiLayer, PartialWasher and BFG Agonizer (with anti-EDR DLL/IAT unhooking) to destroy evidence and brick endpoints. Agrius is the long-running Iranian wiper-masquerading-as-ransomware actor (linked to Apostle/DEADWOOD/SharpBoys). The inventory lists Black Shadow as a separate actor but does NOT contain the Agrius/Agonizing Serpens wiper lineage or this 2023 campaign.
notes: Unit 42 page confirms Agonizing Serpens (aka Agrius), Iranian-linked, Jan-Oct 2023 attacks on Israeli higher-ed/tech with MultiLayer, PartialWasher and BFG Agonizer wipers + PII/IP theft; this Agrius 2023 wiper campaign is absent from the inventory (Black Shadow entries are distinct 2020/2021 leaks).

name: IMPERIAL KITTEN strategic web-compromise campaign against Israeli logistics and tech
supporting_quote: IMPERIAL KITTEN is an Iran-nexus adversary with a suspected connection to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC).
summary: CrowdStrike Counter Adversary Operations attributed an October 2023 wave of strategic web compromise (watering-hole) operations against Israeli transportation, maritime, logistics and technology organizations to IMPERIAL KITTEN (aka Tortoiseshell, TA456, Crimson Sandstorm, Yellow Liderc), an IRGC-suspected Iran-nexus actor active since 2017. Compromised Israeli sites profiled visitors and delivered the IMAPLoader .NET implant (email-based C2 via Yandex accounts); initial access also used job-recruitment social engineering, one-day exploits and stolen VPN credentials. This actor and campaign are NOT in the inventory and add IRGC web-compromise tradecraft to the post-Oct-7 Israel picture.
notes: CrowdStrike page (2023-11-09) confirms IMPERIAL KITTEN as Iran-nexus with suspected IRGC connection, Oct 2023 strategic web compromise vs Israeli transport/logistics/tech, delivering IMAPLoader (moderate-confidence attribution); no existing Imperial Kitten/Tortoiseshell entry in inventory.

name: Peach Sandstorm (APT33) deploys Tickler backdoor against US/UAE defense and satellite sectors
supporting_quote: Microsoft assesses that Peach Sandstorm operates on behalf of the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) based on the group's victimology and operational focus.
summary: Microsoft MSTIC reported that between April and July 2024 the IRGC-attributed actor Peach Sandstorm (APT33 / Refined Kitten / Holmium) deployed a new custom multi-stage backdoor named Tickler against satellite, communications-equipment, oil-and-gas, and federal/state government organizations in the US and UAE. The actor abused fraudulent attacker-controlled Azure subscriptions for C2 and used password-spray plus LinkedIn social engineering and malicious Teams ZIP delivery for initial access. The inventory's APT33 entries are all 2012-2016 Shamoon/Operation-Cleaver era; this is a NEW 2024 APT33 intelligence-gathering operation.
notes: Microsoft page (2024-08-28) confirms Peach Sandstorm = IRGC, Apr-Jul 2024 Tickler custom backdoor vs satellite/comms/oil-gas/gov in US and UAE, fraudulent Azure C2; all existing APT33 inventory entries are 2012-2016, so this 2024 op is new (maps to APT33 actor_id).

name: Curious Serpens (APT33) FalseFont backdoor targets aerospace and defense sector
supporting_quote: The FalseFont backdoor is a highly targeted backdoor, and so far it has been reported to target job applicants in the aerospace and defense industries.
summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 analyzed the FalseFont backdoor used by Curious Serpens (aka Peach Sandstorm, APT33, Elfin, Holmium, Refined Kitten), a suspected Iranian-affiliated actor, against the aerospace and defense sectors (first flagged by Microsoft in Nov-Dec 2023). FalseFont impersonates an aerospace-company HR/job-application portal, steals job-platform credentials, provides remote access, launches additional files and exfiltrates to C2. Confidence set to strongly_inferred because the Unit 42 page hedges on attribution ('suspected Iranian-affiliated'); Microsoft's parallel disclosure attributes it to IRGC-linked Peach Sandstorm. Distinct from the Tickler operation and not in the inventory.
notes: Unit 42 page confirms Curious Serpens (aka Peach Sandstorm/APT33/Elfin/HOLMIUM/REFINED KITTEN), 'suspected Iranian-affiliated', FalseFont backdoor impersonating aerospace HR portal vs aerospace/defense, MS flagged Dec 2023; distinct from Tickler op and absent from inventory. 'suspected Iranian' hedge supports the strongly_inferred label.

name: Lemon Sandstorm / Pioneer Kitten two-year OT pre-positioning intrusion into Middle East CNI
actor: Lemon Sandstorm (Pioneer Kitten / Fox Kitten / UNC757)
supporting_quote: While no confirmed disruption to OT systems was found, FGIR observed targeted reconnaissance and credential harvesting, indicating strong adversary interest in these systems.
summary: FortiGuard's incident-response team disclosed (May 2025) a multi-year Iranian state-sponsored intrusion into a Middle Eastern critical-national-infrastructure entity, active May 2023 to February 2025 (traces to 2021). Initial access via stolen SSL-VPN credentials; the actor deployed custom backdoors HanifNet, HXLibrary, NeoExpressRAT and Havoc, performed extensive lateral movement and targeted reconnaissance/credential harvesting against the OT environment with little data exfiltration, consistent with pre-positioning for a future destructive attack. The Fortinet blog itself attributes only to 'an Iranian state-sponsored threat group'; secondary reporting (The Hacker News, Dark Reading) maps this to Lemon Sandstorm = Pioneer Kitten / Fox Kitten / UNC757, hence strongly_inferred and mapped to the existing Pioneer Kitten actor_id. Distinct OT pre-positioning op not in the inventory.
notes: Fortinet page confirms an Iranian state-sponsored intrusion into a Middle East CNI entity May 2023-Feb 2025 (signs back to 2021), custom backdoors HanifNet/HXLibrary/NeoExpressRAT, OT recon + credential harvesting consistent with pre-positioning; Fortinet attributes only to 'an Iranian state-sponsored threat group' (Lemon/Pioneer Kitten mapping is secondary), correctly justifying strongly_inferred; not in inventory.

name: ANSSI/CERT-FR exposes HOUKEN (UNC5174) Ivanti CSA zero-day campaign against French government, telecom, media, finance and transport
actor: NEW: Houken / UNC5174 cluster (MSS-linked initial-access broker)
summary: On 1 July 2025 ANSSI's CERT-FR published the CTI report 'HOUKEN — Seeking a path by living on the edge with zero-days' (CERTFR-2025-CTI-009), documenting a September 2024 campaign in which an attacker exploited three Ivanti Cloud Service Appliance zero-days (CVE-2024-8190, CVE-2024-8963, CVE-2024-9380) for initial access to French organisations across the governmental, telecommunications, media, finance and transport sectors, with activity continuing to end-November 2024. ANSSI assesses HOUKEN is operated by the same actor MANDIANT tracks as UNC5174 — a Chinese-speaking, UTC+8 initial-access broker that gains footholds (PHP webshells, a kernel rootkit) likely to sell to a state-linked actor seeking intelligence; ANSSI also observed data exfiltration and cryptomining. This is a French-discovered China-nexus access-broker operation NOT in the corpus; UNC5174 is referenced in the CN inventory but has no canonical actor_id, so proposed as a new named cluster. Self-patching of the exploited resources to lock out other actors is a notable TTP.
supporting_quote: In September 2024, ANSSI observed an attack campaign seeking initial access to French entities' networks through the exploitation of several zero-day vulnerabilities on Ivanti Cloud Service Appliance (CSA) devices. French organizations from governmental, telecommunications, media, finance, and transport sectors were impacted.
notes: VERIFIED via pdftotext on the saved PDF (WebFetch's summarizer can't parse PDF bytes, but the file loaded fine): title/ref 'HOUKEN ... CERTFR-2025-CTI-009', Date July 1 2025, all 3 CVEs, Ivanti CSA, Sept 2024, exact sector list, UNC5174 linkage, access-broker-to-state framing, data exfil+cryptominers, UTC+8/Chinese-speaking, self-patching TTP. Supporting quote verbatim. Distinct from inventory's UNC5221 (Ivanti Connect Secure) and UNC5174 VMware Aria entries — different product/CVEs/discloser. Not a duplicate.

name: ANSSI/CERT-FR 'Diplomatic Orbiter' report — Nobelium/Midnight Blizzard (SVR) espionage against French Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Ministry of Culture, ANCT and French embassies
summary: On 19 June 2024 ANSSI's CERT-FR published 'Malicious activities linked to the Nobelium intrusion set' (CERTFR-2024-CTI-006), detailing a sustained SVR campaign (which ANSSI distinguishes as 'Nobelium', publicly described as 'Diplomatic Orbiter') against French and European diplomatic targets since 2021. Named French victims include the Ministry of Culture and the National Agency for Territorial Cohesion (ANCT) — compromised via a 'Strategic Review' lure — plus attempted Cobalt Strike deployment against the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs, and targeting of French embassies in Kyiv, Romania and (impersonation) South Africa. ANSSI publicly links the intrusion set to Russia's SVR foreign-intelligence service. Distinct from the 2020 ANSSI Nobelium/APT29 report already in the corpus; this is a new, France-discovered diplomatic-espionage disclosure. Maps to the existing APT29/Cozy Bear/Midnight Blizzard actor.
supporting_quote: These compromised accounts have been used to target a variety of entities, including the French Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Nobelium operators attempted to install the Cobalt Strike tool on the French Ministry of Foreign Affairs[' systems]
notes: VERIFIED via pdftotext on the saved PDF: title 'MALICIOUS ACTIVITIES LINKED TO THE NOBELIUM INTRUSION SET', dated 2024-06-19; names Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Ministry of Culture + ANCT ('Strategic Review' attachment), Cobalt Strike install attempt vs French MFA, embassies in Kyiv/Romania/South Africa, 'Diplomatic Orbiter', SVR attribution. Distinct from inventory's 2020-01-01 ANSSI Nobelium/APT29 report (different date/named victims). Not a duplicate.

name: France (Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs + ANSSI) formally attributes 2021-2024 espionage against ~10 French entities to APT28 / GRU Unit 26165
summary: On 29 April 2025 France made its first public state attribution of APT28 operations against French entities. ANSSI's CERT-FR released CTI report CERTFR-2025-CTI-007 ('Targeting and compromise of French entities using the APT28 intrusion set'), and the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs condemned the GRU-attributed activity. Since 2021 the intrusion set targeted roughly ten French entities — public services, private companies, and a sports organisation involved in Paris 2024 Olympic preparations, plus defence, aerospace, finance and NGO sectors — using phishing, exploitation of CVE-2023-23397, brute-force against webmail and compromised edge devices. France's foreign ministry explicitly attributes APT28 to Russia's GRU (Unit 26165). Distinct from the 2021 ANSSI APT28 attribution already in the corpus (which covered a 2021-2023 campaign); this 2025 report + foreign-ministry condemnation is a new event. Maps to the existing Fancy Bear / APT28 actor.
supporting_quote: Since 2021, this intrusion set has been used to gather strategic intelligence from entities located in France, Europe, Ukraine, and North America.
notes: VERIFIED via pdftotext on the saved PDF: title 'TARGETING AND COMPROMISE OF FRENCH ENTITIES USING THE APT28 INTRUSION SET', Date April 29 2025, 'since 2021', supporting quote verbatim; TTPs (CVE-2023-23397 0-day, brute-force webmail, edge devices) and sectors (ministerial, DTIB/defence, aerospace, research/think-tanks, financial) confirmed. CAVEAT: the ANSSI PDF attributes APT28 only to 'the Russian Federation' — it does NOT state 'Unit 26165', 'Paris 2024 Olympics/sports org', or 'roughly ten entities'; those derive from the companion MEAE condemnation, not this report (reviewer should soften the summary). Event spine fully supported. Distinct from inventory's 2021-01-01 ANSSI APT28 attribution (2021-2023 campaign). Not a duplicate.

name: NCSC-UK-led Five Eyes advisory on BADBAZAAR and MOONSHINE mobile spyware targeting Uyghur, Tibetan, Taiwanese, Falun Gong and pro-democracy communities for the Chinese state
summary: On 9 April 2025 the UK NCSC, leading a coalition with the FBI, NSA, Australia's ACSC, Canada's CCCS, New Zealand's NCSC and Germany's BfV/BND, published an advisory on two Android/iOS spyware families — BADBAZAAR and MOONSHINE — used to collect microphone, camera, message, photo and location data from mobile devices 'that could be of interest to the Chinese state.' Targets are transnational-repression communities: Uyghurs, Tibetans, Taiwan-independence supporters, Hong Kong/China democracy advocates and Falun Gong, via trojanised apps (e.g. TibetOne, an Uyghur Quran app). Vendor research links MOONSHINE to Poison Carp (Evil Eye/Earth Empusa) and BADBAZAAR to APT15 (Nickel/Vixen Panda). NOT in the corpus; the UK section (17 events) is largely Snowden-era/meta, so this NCSC-led operational disclosure adds a 2025 attested-disclosure data point. NOTE actor_id_existing is a placeholder — the true map is Poison Carp / APT15 (both in the CN inventory but neither has a canonical bracketed actor_id), so a reviewer should re-point this to Poison Carp.
supporting_quote: This advisory includes two case studies detailing techniques used by malicious cyber actors using spyware known as BADBAZAAR and MOONSHINE to target data on mobile devices including smartphones that could be of interest to the Chinese state.
notes: VERIFIED: NCSC page loaded cleanly, dated 9 April 2025; confirms BADBAZAAR+MOONSHINE spyware targeting mobile data 'of interest to the Chinese state', target communities (Taiwan, Tibet, Xinjiang/Uyghur, democracy movements, Falun Gong), and the Five Eyes+Germany coalition (ACSC, CCCS, German BfV/BND, NZ NCSC, FBI, NSA). Supporting quote verbatim. Not in UK or CN inventory; not a duplicate. (actor_id mapping is a reviewer task, not a verification issue.)

name: DOJ/FBI/DCIS DanaBot takedown (Operation Endgame) unseals 16-defendant indictment; espionage variant served Russian state intelligence collection
summary: On 22 May 2025 the US Justice Department, with the FBI Anchorage Field Office and the DoD Inspector General's Defense Criminal Investigative Service, announced — under the Operation Endgame umbrella with German, Dutch and Australian partners — the disruption of the Russia-based DanaBot malware-as-a-service botnet (300,000+ infected machines, USD 50M+ damage) and unsealed a grand-jury indictment and criminal complaint charging 16 individuals. Significantly, a second DanaBot variant targeted military, diplomatic and law-enforcement personnel across North America and Europe, exfiltrating to separate servers; CrowdStrike assesses the operators ('Scully Spider') were acting on behalf of Russian government intelligence-collection interests. Proposed as a US disruption/Defend-Forward enforcement action with a clear state-espionage nexus; NOT in the corpus. Source_url is CyberScoop (the justice.gov press release returned HTTP 403 to the fetcher); the espionage-nexus assessment is vendor (CrowdStrike), hence strongly_inferred rather than attested.
supporting_quote: This variant targeted military officials, diplomats and law enforcement personnel in North America and Europe, sending stolen data to a different server than the fraud-oriented version of DanaBot, officials said.
notes: VERIFIED: CyberScoop article loaded, dated May 22 2025; confirms DOJ/FBI DanaBot C2 seizure under Operation Endgame, 16-defendant indictment, second variant targeting military/diplomats/law-enforcement in NA+Europe, and CrowdStrike's 'Scully Spider acting on behalf of Russian government interests' assessment. Supporting quote verbatim. Distinct from inventory's 2024-05-27 Operation Endgame multi-loader takedown (different date/malware/indictment). Not a duplicate.

name: APT36/TransparentTribe DeskRAT Golang campaign against Indian BOSS Linux military systems
summary: Sekoia TDR disclosed a June–September 2025 TransparentTribe (APT36) campaign delivering a new Golang RAT, DeskRAT, via .desktop-file phishing lures themed on Indian defence matters and Ladakh/New Delhi protests, specifically targeting India's indigenous BOSS Linux distribution used in military and government networks. DeskRAT uses WebSocket C2 and four persistence mechanisms. Distinct from the inventory's generic 2025-04-22 Pahalgam tempo entry: this is a specific named-tooling Linux campaign with a dedicated staging-server infrastructure (modgovindia[.]com). Maps to PK doctrine pk/asymmetric-posture-india.
supporting_quote: TransparentTribe is a Pakistani-nexus intrusion set known to be active since at least 2013 and carrying out cyber espionage operations to support Pakistan military and strategic interests.
notes: Page fully confirms: DeskRAT Golang RAT via .desktop ZIP lures, BOSS Linux targeting, June-Sept 2025, WebSocket C2 (ws://[IP]:8080/ws), four Linux persistence mechanisms (systemd/cron/.desktop autostart/bash), modgovindia[.]com staging, and the Pakistani-nexus quote verbatim. Not in PK inventory (no named Linux/DeskRAT entry). KEEP.

name: DoNot Team LoptikMod espionage campaign against a Southern European foreign-affairs ministry
summary: Trellix Advanced Research Center attributed a spear-phishing campaign against a Southern European foreign-affairs ministry to the India-suspected DoNot Team (APT-C-35 / Origami Elephant). Emails impersonating a defence attache delivered Google Drive RAR archives carrying the group's signature LoptikMod RAT, which establishes scheduled-task persistence and exfiltrates system data. Significant because it is the first documented use of LoptikMod against a major European government entity, marking DoNot's expansion from South Asian regional collection toward European diplomatic intelligence collection. DoNot is already in the IN inventory but this European-MFA event is new; doctrine fit is a stretch beyond the named regional-collection doctrine.
supporting_quote: A threat actor with suspected ties to India has been observed targeting a European foreign affairs ministry with malware capable of harvesting sensitive data from compromised hosts.
notes: Page confirms India-suspected DoNot/APT-C-35 vs a European foreign-affairs ministry, LoptikMod RAT, defence-attache impersonation (Italian attache's Dhaka visit lure), Google Drive RAR delivery, scheduled-task persistence, and 'first documented use of LoptikMod against a major European government entity'. New event (existing IN DoNot entries are South Asian/Android). KEEP.

name: Confucius shifts from WooperStealer to AnonDoor Python backdoor in campaigns against Pakistan
summary: FortiGuard Labs documented the Confucius cyber-espionage group's evolution across December 2024–August 2025 phishing waves against Pakistan, moving from the WooperStealer document/info-stealer (delivered via PPSX and LNK files using DLL side-loading) to AnonDoor, a Python backdoor capable of screenshots, file enumeration, and Chrome password dumping. Confucius is widely assessed as a pro-India / India-aligned actor active since 2013 (per Lookout and Recorded Future reporting), but Fortinet's report itself only characterizes it as a state-sponsored South Asian actor and does not explicitly say pro-India — hence strongly_inferred for the India nexus. Completely new actor: Confucius is not in the inventory. Maps to IN regional-collection-against-Pakistan doctrine.
supporting_quote: Over the past decade, Confucius has repeatedly targeted government agencies, military organizations, defense contractors, and critical industries—especially in Pakistan
notes: Page confirms Confucius WooperStealer-to-AnonDoor (Python backdoor) shift, Dec 2024-Aug 2025, PPSX/LNK + DLL side-loading, screenshots, browser password extraction, and targets in Pakistan; explicitly only 'state-sponsored South Asian' (NOT pro-India), consistent with strongly_inferred. New actor absent from IN inventory. KEEP.

name: Proofpoint/Threatray attribute Bitter (TA397) to the intelligence interests of the Indian state
summary: A two-part Proofpoint + Threatray report assessed with high confidence that TA397/Bitter is a state-backed actor conducting intelligence gathering in the interests of the Indian state, drawing on campaigns from October 2024 to April 2025 against government and diplomatic entities in China, Pakistan and other neighbors, plus recovered Bangladeshi military/tax documents and Indian Standard Time hands-on-keyboard working-hour patterns. Custom toolset includes MiyaRAT, wmRAT, MuuyDownloader and BDarkRAT, with tool-sharing overlaps to Mysterious Elephant (APT-K-47) and Confucius. New actor not in the inventory; this is the first strong public attribution of Bitter to the Indian state. Maps to IN regional-collection doctrine.
supporting_quote: Proofpoint Threat Research assesses it is highly likely that TA397 is a state-backed threat actor tasked with intelligence gathering in the interests of the Indian state.
notes: Page confirms the Indian-state attribution quote verbatim, Oct 2024-April 2025 campaigns, China/Pakistan/South Asian targeting, recovered Bangladeshi military+tax docs, IST 9-5 Mon-Fri working hours, MiyaRAT/wmRAT/BDarkRAT, and tooling overlap with Mysterious Elephant/APT-K-47 and Confucius via ORPCBackdoor. New actor absent from IN inventory. KEEP.

name: Bitter (TA397) spear-phishes Pakistan Telecommunication Company (PTCL) during Operation Sindoor
summary: EclecticIQ reported that on 7 May 2025, amid the India-Pakistan military escalation of India's Operation Sindoor, Bitter APT (TA397) very likely targeted Pakistan Telecommunication Company Limited (PTCL) staff in critical roles (5G engineers, DevOps, satcom experts) with a spear-phishing campaign delivering WmRAT via an IQY/Excel-macro chain, using credentials of Pakistan's Counter Terrorism Department compromised since August 2024 via StealC. A clean conflict-timed example of an India-aligned actor striking Pakistani critical infrastructure. EclecticIQ calls Bitter a "South Asian state-sponsored actor" (not explicitly India) but Proofpoint independently ties TA397 to Indian state interests, so India-nexus is strongly_inferred. New actor; not in inventory.
supporting_quote: On May 7, 2025, during the active military escalation between Pakistan and India—specifically in the context of India's military campaign 'Operation Sindoor'—, EclecticIQ analysts observed that Bitter APT (also known as TA397) very likely targeted the Pakistan Telecommunication Company Limited (PTCL) workers in a spear phishing campaign.
notes: Page confirms May 7 2025 PTCL targeting in the Operation Sindoor context, victims (5G/DevOps/PM/satcom staff), WmRAT via IQY/Excel-macro/CMD chain, and stolen Pakistan CTD credentials from StealC first seen 2024-08-13; Bitter characterized as 'South Asian state-sponsored' (India-nexus strongly_inferred). Distinct from the Proofpoint attribution event. KEEP.

name: Kaspersky GReAT details 2025 Mysterious Elephant (APT-K-47) campaign harvesting WhatsApp data across South Asia
summary: Kaspersky GReAT disclosed a new 2025 Mysterious Elephant (APT-K-47) campaign targeting government and foreign-affairs organizations across Asia-Pacific with a focus on Pakistan, Bangladesh, Afghanistan, Nepal and Sri Lanka, deploying custom tools (BabShell reverse shell, MemLoader HidenDesk) and PowerShell to steal sensitive documents, images, archives and notably WhatsApp data. Mysterious Elephant shares tooling with Bitter (TA397) and Confucius, both India-linked clusters, but Kaspersky does not name a sponsoring state — hence India-nexus is only plausible and filed under IN with that caveat. New actor not in the inventory.
supporting_quote: The group primarily targets government entities and foreign affairs organizations across the Asia-Pacific region, with a focus on Pakistan, Bangladesh, Afghanistan, Nepal, Sri Lanka and other countries.
notes: Page confirms 2025 Mysterious Elephant campaign, Asia-Pacific/Pakistan-Bangladesh-Afghanistan-Nepal-Sri Lanka focus, BabShell + MemLoader HidenDesk + PowerShell tooling, and theft of documents/images/archives/WhatsApp data. Press release does NOT name a sponsoring state, does NOT use the APT-K-47 label, and does NOT cite Bitter/Confucius overlaps — fully consistent with the candidate's 'plausible' India-nexus caveat. New actor absent from inventory. KEEP.

name: APT36 NIC/Kavach credential-phishing campaign against Indian defense personnel (post-Pahalgam)
summary: CYFIRMA documented an APT36 (Transparent Tribe) credential-stealing phishing campaign against Indian defense personnel, using PDFs that imitate the National Informatics Centre (NIC) login portal and harvest email passwords plus Kavach 2FA codes via spoofed domains, with Pahalgam-terror-attack-themed lures. Distinct, named credential-harvesting operation supporting long-term infiltration of Indian defense networks — separate from the inventory's generic 2025-04-22 tempo entry and from the DeskRAT Linux campaign. Maps to PK doctrine pk/asymmetric-posture-india.
supporting_quote: APT36, also known as Transparent Tribe, is a Pakistan-based cyber espionage group that has been actively targeting Indian defense personnel through highly sophisticated phishing campaigns.
notes: CORE CLAIM SUPPORTED: page (dated 2025-06-21) confirms APT36/Transparent Tribe as Pakistan-based credential-phishing against Indian defense via PDFs imitating the NIC login portal (spoof domain SuperPrimeServices.com). CAVEAT: page does NOT mention Kavach 2FA harvesting or Pahalgam-themed lures — those summary specifics are unsupported and should be dropped; downgrade confidence accordingly. Distinct from the generic 2025-04-22 tempo entry and the Sekoia DeskRAT/Aryaka cross-platform events. KEEP (with corrected summary).

name: APT36 and SideCopy cross-platform (Windows/Linux) RAT campaigns against Indian defense — Geta RAT, Ares RAT, DeskRAT
summary: Aryaka (Aditya K. Sood) detailed cross-platform RAT campaigns by Pakistan-aligned APT36 and its SideCopy subdivision against Indian defense and government-aligned organizations, compromising both Windows and Linux via phishing that drops LNK files, ELF binaries and PowerPoint Add-Ins to deliver Geta RAT, Ares RAT and DeskRAT for data theft and persistent access. Documents the maturing cross-platform ecosystem of the SideCopy/APT36 pair into early 2026. Both actors already in the PK inventory, but this consolidated cross-platform Geta/Ares-RAT campaign is a new event; doctrine fit pk/asymmetric-posture-india. Confidence strongly_inferred (single vendor blog, no government corroboration).
supporting_quote: Indian defense sector and government-aligned organizations have been targeted by multiple campaigns that are designed to compromise Windows and Linux environments with remote access trojans capable of stealing sensitive data and ensuring continued access to infected machines.
notes: Page (2026-02-11, attributing Aryaka/Aditya K. Sood) confirms Pakistan-aligned APT36 + SideCopy, Indian defense/government targets, Windows+Linux compromise, and Geta RAT / Ares RAT (Python) / DeskRAT delivered via LNK (mshta), ELF/Go binaries, and PowerPoint Add-Ins. New consolidated cross-platform event distinct from the Sekoia DeskRAT and CYFIRMA NIC entries. KEEP.

name: Elastic discloses APT32 PHOREAL backdoor against a Vietnamese financial institution
summary: Elastic Security Labs (intrusion set REF4322) detailed an APT32/OceanLotus operation deploying the PHOREAL/RIZZO backdoor against a Vietnamese financial-services institution, loading it into memory for defense evasion. Extends the inventory's APT32 record (which stops at 2021) into 2023 and into the domestic financial sector, consistent with Vietnam's industrial/economic cyber-collection posture.
supporting_quote: We assess with high confidence based on observed activity and previous reporting that REF4322 is APT32/OceanLotus and the actor behind this incident.
notes: Page confirms REF4322=APT32/OceanLotus (high confidence), PHOREAL/RIZZO backdoor, Vietnamese financial-services target, March 2023; not in VN inventory (APT32 stops 2021).

name: Elastic discloses APT32 SPECTRALVIPER backdoor against a Vietnamese agribusiness (REF2754)
summary: Elastic Security Labs disclosed intrusion set REF2754 targeting a large Vietnamese agribusiness of national food-production importance with previously-undocumented tooling: the SPECTRALVIPER x64 backdoor plus the P8LOADER loader and POWERSEAL PowerShell runner. Elastic linked the activity to APT32/OceanLotus/Canvas Cyclone and a Vietnamese state-affiliated threat, evidencing domestic strategic-sector collection. New event (inventory APT32 record ends 2021).
supporting_quote: Elastic Security Labs concludes with moderate confidence that this campaign is executed by a Vietnamese state-affiliated threat.
notes: Page confirms REF2754 vs a systemically-important Vietnamese agribusiness, SPECTRALVIPER/P8LOADER/POWERSEAL, moderate-confidence Vietnamese state-affiliated overlapping Canvas Cyclone/APT32/OceanLotus, June 2023; distinct from PHOREAL, not in inventory.

name: Huntress exposes multi-year APT32 intrusion of a Vietnamese human-rights non-profit
summary: Huntress detailed an APT32/OceanLotus intrusion of a non-profit supporting Vietnamese human rights that persisted for at least four years (initial persistence to June 2020), using scheduled tasks masquerading as Adobe/Windows updaters, COM-object hijacking, DLL side-loading, PNG steganography and Cobalt Strike. Extends the inventory's documented APT32 transnational-repression / dissident-surveillance pattern into 2024.
supporting_quote: The target was a non-profit supporting Vietnamese human rights.
notes: Page confirms intrusion of a non-profit supporting Vietnamese human rights, ~4-yr persistence from June 2020, Adobe/Windows-update scheduled tasks, COM hijack, DLL side-load, PNG steganography, Cobalt Strike, and APT32/OceanLotus overlap incl. a dedicated YARA match; distinct 2024 disclosure (not the 2018 Amnesty event).

name: Citizen Lab discloses Paragon Graphite mercenary spyware targeting journalists and civil society
actor: NEW: Paragon Solutions (Graphite) — Israeli Unit-8200-founded commercial spyware vendor
summary: Citizen Lab published the first technical look at Paragon Solutions' Graphite spyware, an Israeli firm founded by ex-PM Ehud Barak and former Unit 8200 commander Ehud Schneorson. WhatsApp notified ~90 journalists and civil-society members of a zero-click Graphite campaign (notification 2025-01-31); confirmed Italian victims include Fanpage editor Francesco Cancellato and migrant-rescue activists (infections 2024-12-14, later corroborated by Italian prosecutors). A new Israeli commercial-spyware actor extending the IL slice beyond Predatory Sparrow / Mossad and the QME / counter-Iran doctrines toward export-led offensive capability.
supporting_quote: On January 31, 2025, WhatsApp sent notifications to approximately 90 WhatsApp accounts they believed were targeted with Paragon's spyware, including journalists and members of civil society.
notes: Page confirms Paragon (Israeli; founders Ehud Barak + ex-Unit-8200 Ehud Schneorson), Graphite spyware, WhatsApp's Jan 31 2025 notification of ~90 journalists/civil-society accounts, and victim Francesco Cancellato of Fanpage.it; no Paragon/commercial-spyware actor in the 8-event IL inventory.

name: Abu Dhabi Secrets — UAE-financed pan-European surveillance and smear campaign via Alp Services
actor: NEW: UAE / Alp Services (Ariaf Studies and Research) influence-and-surveillance cluster
summary: Mediapart and the European Investigative Collaborations (EIC), working from ~78,000 documents hacked from Swiss intelligence firm Alp Services, exposed that the UAE government (via front entity Ariaf Studies and Research, ~EUR 5.7M, 2017-2020) contracted Alp to surveil and smear more than 1,000 individuals and 400 organizations across 18 European countries as alleged Muslim Brotherhood affiliates, including French public figures. Triggered French and Swiss criminal investigations. A new, well-documented UAE state-directed transnational influence/surveillance operation absent from the inventory.
supporting_quote: Alp Services has been contracted by the UAE government to spy on citizens of 18 countries in Europe and beyond.
notes: EIC hub page attests UAE government contracted Alp Services to spy across 18 European countries and flagged 1,000+ individuals / 400 orgs as Muslim Brotherhood to UAE intelligence; the ~78,000-documents and ~EUR 5.7M figures are NOT on this hub page (likely in linked partner articles). Core actor/event/date supported; not in AE inventory.

name: South Korea publishes 2024 National Cybersecurity Strategy adopting an offensive posture toward North Korea
actor: NEW: Republic of Korea National Security Office / Office of the President (cyber strategy)
summary: The Office of the President released South Korea's National Cybersecurity Strategy in February 2024, explicitly shifting from the 2019 defensive posture to a 'proactive/offensive' response paradigm and naming North Korea as the primary threat, mirroring the US 'defend forward' concept. A doctrine-publication milestone for the thinnest state in the corpus (KR, 6 events) and a candidate successor/update to the kr/ncss-2019 doctrine. Meta/announcement (lower priority) but doctrinally load-bearing.
supporting_quote: reinforcing our defense capabilities has its limits. Therefore, we must change our paradigm to respond offensively to North Korea and other threats
notes: CSIS primer confirms Feb 2024 release by the Office of the President, paradigm shift from 2019 defensive to offensive posture, North Korea named primary threat, and 'defend forward' parallel; KR inventory has the 2019 NCSS but not the 2024 strategy.

name: FBI/CISA #StopRansomware: Akira advisory — $244M, imminent threat to critical infrastructure
summary: FBI, CISA, DC3, HHS, Europol and the national cyber authorities of France, Germany and the Netherlands updated their 2024 joint advisory (AA24-109A) on Akira ransomware, warning it now presents an imminent threat to critical infrastructure. Akira, first identified March 2023, has collected more than $244M in ransom proceeds as of late September 2025 and is assessed by the FBI as a top-five ransomware variant. The group is linked to Storm-1567 / Howling Scorpius / Punk Spider / Gold Sahara clusters and has possible Conti lineage; recent intrusions pivoted to exploiting SonicWall SSL-VPN devices (CVE-2024-40766) and encrypting Nutanix AHV in addition to VMware ESXi/Hyper-V. Akira is a major gap in the corpus (no existing events) and is one of the most prolific RaaS operations of 2024–2025.
supporting_quote: Akira ransomware has claimed more than $244 million in ransomware proceeds as of late September, the FBI and Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security agency said in the joint advisory.
notes: CONFIRMED: page (pub 2025-11-13) states $244M as of late September, March 2023 origin, FBI top-five, joint advisory by FBI/CISA/Europol/France/Germany/Netherlands; six new CVEs incl. SonicWall. Page does NOT list DC3/HHS as co-authors (minor summary overreach). Akira absent from corpus — genuine new actor.

name: FBI/CISA/ASD #StopRansomware: Play (Playcrypt) advisory — ~900 affected entities
summary: CISA, FBI and Australia's ASD's ACSC updated their joint advisory AA23-352A on Play (Playcrypt) ransomware. As of May 2025 the FBI had identified approximately 900 affected entities, up from ~300 in the original December 2023 advisory, making Play among the most prolific ransomware groups of 2024. Active since June 2022, Play gains access via valid accounts bought on criminal markets and exploitation of public-facing apps (FortiOS, Microsoft Exchange ProxyNotShell/OWASSRF). Play is a major gap in the corpus (no existing events). The double/multi-extortion model and the breadth of North/South American and European critical-infrastructure victims make it doctrinally relevant to the e-crime slice.
supporting_quote: As of May 2025, the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) had identified approximately 900 affected entities believed to have been compromised by Play ransomware operators.
notes: CONFIRMED: AttackIQ page confirms updated AA23-352A (CISA/FBI/ASD ACSC), ~900 affected entities as of May 2025, active since June 2022, ProxyNotShell/OWASSRF exploitation, June 4 2025 update. Play absent from corpus — genuine new actor.

name: FBI/CISA/HHS/MS-ISAC #StopRansomware: Interlock advisory — healthcare-focused double extortion
summary: CISA, FBI, HHS and MS-ISAC issued joint advisory AA25-203A on the Interlock ransomware-as-a-service operation, which first emerged in late September 2024 and disproportionately targets healthcare and critical-infrastructure organizations across North America and Europe. Interlock uses a double-extortion model with Windows and Linux/ESXi encryptors and an uncommon initial-access technique — drive-by downloads of fake software installers from compromised legitimate websites, plus the ClickFix social-engineering method. Interlock is not currently in the corpus and fills the 2024–2025 healthcare-extortion gap alongside Qilin and RansomHub.
supporting_quote: Interlock is a ransomware-as-a-service operation that first emerged in September 2024.
notes: CONFIRMED: page (pub 2025-07-23) confirms joint alert by CISA/FBI/HHS/MS-ISAC, RaaS emerged Sept 2024, healthcare focus, double extortion, drive-by fake Chrome/Edge installers, ClickFix. (Advisory dated 7/23 vs candidate 7/22 — 1-day variance, negligible.) Interlock absent from corpus.

name: Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters launch Salesforce data-leak/extortion site (39 victims, ~1B records)
summary: A coalition self-branded 'Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters' — drawing members from ShinyHunters, Scattered Spider and Lapsus$ — launched a dedicated data-leak site on 3 October 2025 to extort 39 companies whose Salesforce environments were compromised via voice-phishing and malicious-OAuth abuse (including the Salesloft Drift integration). The group claimed ~1 billion stolen records and demanded a ransom from Salesforce itself, setting an October 10 deadline; named victims include FedEx, Disney/Hulu, Home Depot, Marriott, Google, Cisco, Toyota, McDonald's, Adidas, Qantas, Allianz Life and Workday. This formalizes the UNC6040/ShinyHunters Salesforce campaign already in the corpus into a named, unified e-crime brand and is a distinct leak-site event. Maps onto existing ShinyHunters / Scattered Spider / UNC6040 actors.
supporting_quote: Today, they launched a new data leak site containing 39 companies impacted by the attacks.
notes: CONFIRMED: page (pub 2025-10-03) confirms 'Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters' launched leak portal with 39 companies, ~1B records, Oct 10 deadline, direct ransom demand to Salesforce, Salesloft Drift / OAuth / vishing. DISTINCT from corpus: existing entries are the individual victim data-thefts (Apr–Aug 2025) and the UNC6040 umbrella; the Oct 3 leak-site launch + extortion of Salesforce itself is a separate hack-and-leak event. Not a duplicate.

name: Jaguar Land Rover cyberattack — five-week production halt, claimed by Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters
summary: On 31 August 2025 Jaguar Land Rover suffered a cyberattack that forced it to pause all global production on 1 September; the outage ran for over a month, with weekly costs estimated at ~£50M and an assessed total economic impact on the UK of roughly £1.9 billion — described as possibly the most damaging cyberattack in British history. A group calling itself 'Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters' (a Scattered Spider / Lapsus$ / ShinyHunters confederation) claimed responsibility on Telegram; initial access was via a vishing campaign harvesting employee credentials. Not in the corpus; a flagship 2025 e-crime disruption event with severe cyber-physical/economic spillover. Maps onto existing Scattered Spider / ShinyHunters actors.
supporting_quote: Shortly after the attack, a group calling itself Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters on Telegram claimed responsibility for the attack.
notes: CONFIRMED: Wikipedia confirms attack began 31 Aug 2025, production halted 1 Sept for weeks, 'Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters' Telegram claim, ~£50M/week, ~£1.9B UK economic impact, 'most damaging cyberattack in British history.' Vishing-initial-access detail NOT on this page (not load-bearing). No JLR event in corpus — distinct.

name: Black Basta internal chat-log leak (ExploitWhispers) exposes Russia-based operation
summary: On 11 February 2025 a leaker using the alias 'ExploitWhispers' published more than 200,000 internal Russian-language chat messages (spanning 18 Sep 2023 – 28 Sep 2024) of the Black Basta ransomware gang to Prodaft and on Telegram. The leaker said Black Basta had 'crossed the line' by attacking Russian domestic banks, motivating the disclosure; the logs confirm a prolific Russian-language operation with Moscow offices and a tacit expectation of protection by Russian authorities, and reference earlier blockchain tracing of at least $107M in Bitcoin ransoms since early 2022. Black Basta is a singleton in the corpus (only the 2024 Ascension Health event); this is a high-leverage second event that also evidences the Russia tolerance-of-e-crime nexus.
supporting_quote: The chat logs, which include over 200,000 messages spanning from September 18, 2023, to September 28, 2024, were shared with threat intelligence company Prodaft by a leaker.
notes: CONFIRMED: TechCrunch (pub 2025-02-21) confirms ExploitWhispers alias, 200k+ messages Sep 18 2023–Sep 28 2024, shared with Prodaft, motivation = targeting Russian banks ('crossed the line'), Russian-language gang. $107M Bitcoin figure NOT in this source (not load-bearing). Distinct from the corpus's sole Black Basta event (2024 Ascension Health) — second event for a singleton actor.

name: Cl0p mass extortion via Oracle E-Business Suite zero-day (CVE-2025-61882)
summary: Beginning 29 September 2025, Google Threat Intelligence Group and Mandiant tracked a large-scale extortion campaign by an actor using the CL0P brand, sending high-volume emails to executives at numerous organizations claiming theft of data from their Oracle E-Business Suite environments. The campaign exploited an unauthenticated-RCE zero-day (CVE-2025-61882, CVSS 9.8) in the EBS BI Publisher/Concurrent Processing component, with exploitation as early as August 2025 (and suspicious activity back to July 10). This is Cl0p's signature mass-MFT/enterprise-app extortion pattern (cf. MOVEit, GoAnywhere, Cleo) applied to a new target and is distinct from the 2024 Cleo campaign already in the corpus. Maps onto the existing Cl0p / TA505 / FIN11 actor.
supporting_quote: Beginning Sept. 29, 2025, Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) and Mandiant began tracking a new, large-scale extortion campaign by a threat actor claiming affiliation with the CL0P extortion brand.
notes: CONFIRMED: GTIG/Mandiant blog confirms Sept 29 2025 campaign start, CL0P brand, high-volume executive emails, Oracle EBS, CVE-2025-61882 exploited as zero-day from Aug 9 2025 with activity back to July 10 2025. CVSS 9.8 not explicit on page (not load-bearing). Distinct from corpus's 2024 Cl0p Cleo campaign — new target, separate event.

name: Noah Urban (Scattered Spider) sentenced to 10 years — first conviction of a group member
summary: On 21 August 2025 Noah Michael Urban, a 20-year-old from Palm Coast, Florida, was sentenced to 10 years in federal prison and ordered to pay $13M in restitution — the first conviction of a member of Scattered Spider, the crew the court tied to breaches of more than 130 major companies (Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp, Caesars, MGM). He had pleaded guilty in April 2025 to wire-fraud and aggravated-identity-theft charges across Florida and California cases. The corpus already lists Urban's 2022 SIM-swap charge and the Nov 2024 five-operator indictment; this sentencing is a distinct, milestone law-enforcement outcome. Maps onto the existing Scattered Spider actor.
supporting_quote: A 20-year-old Florida man received a 10-year federal prison sentence Wednesday for his role in the notorious Scattered Spider cybercrime organization, marking the first conviction of a member from the group responsible for breaching more than 130 major companies.
notes: CONFIRMED: CyberScoop (pub 2025-08-21) confirms 120-month sentence, $13M restitution, 'first conviction of a member' of Scattered Spider, 130+ companies, Twilio/LastPass/DoorDash/Mailchimp/Caesars/MGM, guilty plea to conspiracy/wire-fraud/aggravated-ID-theft. April 2025 plea month not on page (not load-bearing). Distinct from corpus's 2022 Urban charge and 2024 five-operator indictment — this is the sentencing/conviction.
